title: oxalis albicans subsp. pilosa
intro: Oxalis albicans subsp. pilosa, also known as the desert sweetroot or purple wood sorrel, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the wood sorrel family (Oxalidaceae). Traditionally, this plant has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Oxalis albicans subsp. pilosa include its potential to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections. The plant can be prepared into various herbal forms, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to harness its medicinal properties.
